name: lingua-py (Accurate Language Detection)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Peter Stahl; Python bindings to Rust core

performance.notes: Rust core with Python bindings; very fast; model loading takes ~100ms at startup

agent_readiness.notes: Accurate natural language detection for 75 languages including rare/minority ones. Rust core with Python bindings. Significantly more accurate than langdetect for short texts. Builder pattern for detector configuration: high/low accuracy modes, whitelist/blacklist languages. Returns confidence values. Supports: African languages, Indigenous languages, and languages with similar scripts that trip up simpler tools. Used when accuracy matters more than minimal dependencies.

gotchas: Model loading at startup adds latency (~100ms)
High accuracy mode is slower than low accuracy mode
Rust extension means binary wheel required (available for common platforms)
best_when: Accurate detection of 75 languages including rare/minority
Short text detection where langdetect fails
Production pipelines requiring deterministic, accurate language ID
avoid_when: Embedded systems with tight memory constraints
Need only a few major languages (simpler tools sufficient)
summary: lingua-py is the most accurate language detection library for Python, covering 75 languages with a Rust core for performance. Significantly outperforms langdetect on short texts and rare languages. Builder API for configuring precision, whitelists, and blacklists. Deterministic results. Best choice when detection accuracy matters — especially for short texts, similar-script languages, and minority languages.
